```{r, echo=TRUE}
    T_cap <- Tq * infl

    # Broadcast if needed
    if (length(T_cap) == 1L) T_cap <- rep(T_cap, length(t_obs))
    if (length(T_cap) != length(t_obs)) T_cap <- rep(T_cap[1L], length(t_obs))

  } else {
    stop("cap_base must be 'perm' or 'tmax'.")
  }

  # Guardrails: enforce positive finite caps
  bad <- !is.finite(T_cap) | T_cap <= 0
  if (any(bad)) {
    fallback <- max(T_cap[!bad], 1)
    T_cap[bad] <- fallback
  }
  T_cap
}

# Standardized Lifted Log-Saturation (SLLS) using unified Z-cap
# - Standardize to Z using permutation mean/sd per test
# - Build the cap on the Z-scale via .build_Tcap (cap_base = "perm" or "tmax")
# - Apply simplified LLS on Z
# - Map epsilon back to T-scale via sigma_j
eps_log_saturation_lift_std <- function(obs_stats,
                                        perm_stats,
                                        sampsize,
                                        cap_base      = c("perm", "tmax"),  # Z-cap choice
                                        alpha         = 0.5, # g(n) = max(1, alpha*sqrt(n))
                                        q_ref         = 0.99, # quantile of |Z_perm|
                                        rho_lift      = 3,
                                        k_factor      = 1000,
                                        target_factor = 0.25,
                                        floor_epsZ    = 1e-6,
                                        ...) {
  cap_base <- if (is.character(cap_base)) match.arg(cap_base) else cap_base
  n <- sampsize
  stopifnot(n > 0)

  # Shape inputs --------------------------------------------------------------
  # Allow vector perm_stats for single test
  if (is.null(dim(perm_stats))) perm_stats <- matrix(perm_stats, ncol = 1)
  Tobs <- as.numeric(obs_stats)

  # Z-standardization per test -----------------------------------------------
  mu  <- colMeans(perm_stats, na.rm = TRUE)
  sig <- apply(perm_stats, 2, sd, na.rm = TRUE)

  # Guard against non-finite or zero SDs: replace with a small positive fallback
  valid_sig <- sig[is.finite(sig) & sig > 0]
  sig_fallback <- if (length(valid_sig)) max(min(valid_sig), 1e-12) else 1e-12
  sig_ok <- sig
  sig_ok[!is.finite(sig_ok) | sig_ok <= 0] <- sig_fallback

  Zobs  <- abs((Tobs - mu) / sig_ok)
  Zperm <- sweep(sweep(perm_stats, 2, mu, "-"), 2, sig_ok, "/")  # same shape as perm_stats

  # Build Z-cap (unified) -----------------------------------------------------
  # Uses: "tmax" => max(Zobs); "perm" => Q_q(|Zperm|) * g(n) with g(n)=max(1, alpha*sqrt(n))
  T_cap_Z <- .build_Tcap(cap_base = cap_base,
                         t_obs    = Zobs,
                         perm_stats = Zperm,
                         n        = n,
                         q_ref    = q_ref,
                         alpha    = alpha)

  # LLS core on Z -------------------------------------------------------------
  k        <- k_factor      * (500 / n)
  E_target <- target_factor * (500 / n)

  if (!any(is.finite(T_cap_Z)) || all(T_cap_Z <= 0)) {
    # Conservative constant if the cap failed
    epsZ <- rep(max(E_target + rho_lift, floor_epsZ), length(Zobs))
  } else {
    s   <- pmin(Zobs / T_cap_Z, 1)        # scale-free index in [0,1]
    psi <- (1 - s)^4 * (1 + 4*s)          # Wendland C^2 lift
    epsZ_core <- E_target * log1p(k * s) / log1p(k) + rho_lift * psi
    epsZ <- pmax(epsZ_core, floor_epsZ)
  }

  # Map back to T-scale -------------------------------------------------------
  epsT <- epsZ * sig_ok
  epsT
}
```


```{r rule_log_sat_lift_std}
for (n_val in n_per_group_vec[1:2]) {
  obs_stats <- readRDS(path_target(paste0("obs_t_stats_n", n_val, "_B", B, ".rds")))
  perm_stats <- readRDS(path_target(paste0("perm_t_stats_n", n_per_group, "_B", B, ".rds")))
  
  p <- tibble(
    t_obs = abs(obs_stats),
    eps_n2 = eps_n_power(obs_stats, sampsize = n_val),
    eps_log_sat_lift_perm = 
      eps_log_saturation_lift_cap(obs_stats, perm_stats = perm_stats, 
                                  sampsize = n_val, cap_base = "perm"),
    eps_log_sat_lift_std = 
      eps_log_saturation_lift_std(obs_stats, perm_stats = perm_stats, 
                                  sampsize = n_val, cap_base = "perm")
  ) %>%
    pivot_longer(-t_obs, names_to = "rule", values_to = "eps") %>%
    ggplot(aes(t_obs, eps, color = rule)) +
    geom_line(size = 1.2, alpha = 0.7) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Epsilon scaling comparison (n = ", n_val, ")"),
      x = expression(abs(t[obs])),
      y = expression(epsilon)
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  print(p)
}
```

Since the data in this study are already standardized (Gaussian data with sd = 1), there is almost no difference in epsilon values between the standardized and non-standardized version. We will see a difference in a later study using the Wilcoxon test.

```{r run_permApprox_constr_log_sat_lift_std}
results_all <- run_permApprox(rule_name = "log_sat_lift_std", 
                              eps_fct = eps_log_saturation_lift_std)
```


```{r pvals_constr_log_sat_lift_std, fig.width=10, fig.height=5, dpi=200}
plot_list <- lapply(n_per_group_vec, function(n_val) {
  df_n <- results_all %>% 
    filter(n_per_group == n_val, gpd_fit == TRUE)
  
  plot_pvals(
    df = df_n,
    sampsize = n_val,
    eps_rule = "standardized lifted log-saturation (SLLS)"
  ) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
})

wrap_plots(plotlist = plot_list, ncol = 3, guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(title = "Epsilon rule: standardized lifted log-saturation (SLLS)") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r pvals_constr_log_sat_lift_std_3n, fig.width=10, fig.height=3.2, dpi=200}
n_show <- c(50, 100, 500)

# top row: p vs t
plots_pt <- lapply(n_show, function(n_val) {
  df_n <- results_all %>% filter(n_per_group == n_val, gpd_fit == TRUE)
  
  plot_pvals(
    df = df_n,
    sampsize = n_val,
    eps_rule = "standardized lifted log-saturation (SLLS)"
  ) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
})

((plots_pt[[1]] | plots_pt[[2]] | plots_pt[[3]])) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```


```{r pvals_constr_log_sat_lift_std_pp, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, dpi=200}
# bottom row: p vs p
plots_pp <- lapply(n_show, function(n_val) {
  plot_pval_agreement_n(
    df = results_all %>% filter(gpd_fit == TRUE),
    sampsize = n_val
  ) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
})

((plots_pt[[1]] | plots_pt[[2]] | plots_pt[[3]]) /
 (plots_pp[[1]] | plots_pp[[2]] | plots_pp[[3]])) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
eval_df <- prepare_rule_eval(results_all, rule_name = "log_sat_lift_std")

summ_list <- make_rule_summary(eval_df)

# Mean absolute log10 error table
tab_abs_log10_mae <- make_wide_metric_table(
  summ_list$by_setting,
  metric = "mean_abs_log10_err",
  digits = 3
)

print_metric_table(
  tab_abs_log10_mae,
  caption = "Mean absolute log10 error for epsilon rule: log_sat_lift_std"
)
```

```{r heat_slls,fig.width=7, fig.height=5, dpi=200}
plot_metric_heatmap(
  summ_list$by_setting,
  metric = "mean_abs_log10_err",
  title = "Performance heatmap: log_sat_lift_std",
  facet_rule = FALSE
)
```

## Constrained vs. unconstrained fit

```{r}
# Load & combine results: t-test, unconstrained GPD, constrained GPD
rule_name <- "log_sat_lift_std"   # <- change if you used a different rule_name

results_three <- purrr::map_dfr(n_per_group_vec, function(n_per_group) {

  # base inputs
  obs_stats   <- readRDS(path_target(paste0("obs_t_stats_n", n_per_group, 
                                            "_B", B, ".rds")))
  pval_ttest  <- readRDS(path_target(paste0("pval_ttest_n", n_per_group, 
                                            "_B", B, ".rds")))

  # GPD results
  papprox_unconstr <- readRDS(path_target(paste0("permapprox_unconstr_n", 
                                                 n_per_group, "_B", B, ".rds")))
  papprox_constr   <- readRDS(path_target(paste0("permapprox_constr_",
                                                 rule_name, "_n", n_per_group, 
                                                 "_B", B, ".rds")))

  tibble::tibble(
    idx           = seq_along(obs_stats),
    n_per_group   = n_per_group,
    effect_size   = factor(delta_vec, levels = c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2)),
    obs_stats     = abs(obs_stats),
    p_ttest       = pval_ttest,
    p_unconstr    = papprox_unconstr$p_unadjusted,
    p_constr      = papprox_constr$p_unadjusted,
    gpd_unconstr_fit = papprox_unconstr$gpd_fit$fitted,
    gpd_constr_fit   = papprox_constr$gpd_fit$fitted
  )
})
```


```{r}
# Plot function: three p-value types in one panel
plot_pvals_three <- function(df, sampsize, log_scale = TRUE) {
  df_plot <- df

  # find smallest nonzero across all three types for stable zero replacement on log scale
  min_nonzero <- min(
    c(df_plot$p_ttest[df_plot$p_ttest > 0],
      df_plot$p_unconstr[df_plot$p_unconstr > 0],
      df_plot$p_constr[df_plot$p_constr > 0]),
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
  min_nonzero <- max(min_nonzero, 1e-320)
  zero_repl   <- min_nonzero / 100
  
  df_long <- df_plot %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      p_ttest_plot     = ifelse(p_ttest     == 0, zero_repl, p_ttest),
      p_unconstr_plot  = ifelse(p_unconstr  == 0, zero_repl, p_unconstr),
      p_constr_plot    = ifelse(p_constr    == 0, zero_repl, p_constr)
    ) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols = c(p_ttest_plot, p_unconstr_plot, p_constr_plot),
      names_to = "ptype", values_to = "pval"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      ptype = dplyr::recode(ptype,
                            p_ttest_plot    = "t-test",
                            p_unconstr_plot = "unconstrained",
                            p_constr_plot   = "constrained"
      ),
      # set the legend order
      ptype = factor(ptype, levels = c("t-test", "constrained", "unconstrained"))
    ) %>%
    # this controls the draw order in a single geom_point layer
    dplyr::arrange(ptype)
  
  p <- ggplot2::ggplot(df_long, ggplot2::aes(x = obs_stats, y = pval, color = ptype, size = ptype)) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
      name   = "P-value type:",
      values = c("t-test" = "grey40", "unconstrained" = "#1f77b4", 
                 "constrained" = "darkorange")
    ) + 
    scale_size_manual(
      values = c("t-test" = 1.6,
                 "constrained" = 1.6,
                 "unconstrained" = 1.2)  # smaller dots here
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = paste0("n = ", sampsize),
      x = expression(abs(t[obs])),
      y = expression(p)
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
    ggplot2::theme(
      axis.text    = ggplot2::element_text(size = 14),
      axis.title   = ggplot2::element_text(size = 16),
      plot.title   = ggplot2::element_text(size = 16),
      legend.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 14),
      legend.text  = ggplot2::element_text(size = 12)
    ) +
    guides(size = "none")

  if (log_scale) {
    p <- p +
      ggplot2::scale_y_log10(
        breaks = c(zero_repl, 1e-300, 1e-200, 1e-100, 1e-80, 1e-60, 1e-40, 1e-20, 1e-2),
        labels = c("0", "1e-300", "1e-200", "1e-100", "1e-80", "1e-60", "1e-40", "1e-20", "1e-2"),
        limits = c(zero_repl, 1),
        minor_breaks = NULL
      )
  }

  return(p)
}
```


```{r pvals_unconstr_vs_constr, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, dpi=200}
# Make the plots for each sample size
for (n_val in n_per_group_vec) {
  df_n <- dplyr::filter(results_three, n_per_group == n_val)
  cat("n =", n_val,
      "| zeros (ttest, unconst, const):",
      sum(df_n$p_ttest == 0, na.rm = TRUE), ",",
      sum(df_n$p_unconstr == 0, na.rm = TRUE), ",",
      sum(df_n$p_constr == 0, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  print(plot_pvals_three(df_n, sampsize = n_val))
}

```

## Combined plot

```{r}
results_fact0  <- run_permApprox(rule_name = "factor0", eps_fct = eps_factor)
results_fact01 <- run_permApprox(rule_name = "factor01", eps_fct = eps_factor)
results_n2     <- run_permApprox(rule_name = "n_squared", eps_fct = eps_n_power)
results_ls     <- run_permApprox(rule_name = "log_sat", 
                                 eps_fct = eps_log_saturation)
results_slls   <- run_permApprox(rule_name = "log_sat_lift_std", 
                                 eps_fct = eps_log_saturation_lift_std)
```

```{r eps_rules_combined, fig.width=10, fig.height=6, dpi=200}
sampsize <- 250

p_fact0 <- results_fact0  %>% dplyr::filter(n_per_group == sampsize) %>%
  plot_pvals(sampsize = sampsize, title = "ε = 0")

p_fact01 <- results_fact01 %>% dplyr::filter(n_per_group == sampsize) %>%
  plot_pvals(sampsize = sampsize, title = "ε-factor = 0.1")

p_ls <- results_ls %>% dplyr::filter(n_per_group == sampsize) %>%
  plot_pvals(sampsize = sampsize, title = "Log-saturation")

p_slls <- results_slls %>% dplyr::filter(n_per_group == sampsize) %>%
  plot_pvals(sampsize = sampsize, title = "Std. lifted log-saturation (SLLS)")

# All four plots
(p_fact0 | p_fact01) /
(p_ls    | p_slls) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

```

```{r eps_rules_combined_six, fig.width=10, fig.height=8, dpi=200}
sampsize <- 250

p_fact0 <- results_fact0  %>% dplyr::filter(n_per_group == sampsize) %>%
  plot_pvals(sampsize = sampsize, title = "ε = 0") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 16))

p_fact01 <- results_fact01 %>% dplyr::filter(n_per_group == sampsize) %>%
  plot_pvals(sampsize = sampsize, title = "ε-factor = 0.1") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 16))

p_n2_100 <- results_n2 %>% dplyr::filter(n_per_group == 100) %>%
  plot_pvals(sampsize = sampsize, title = "Power-law in n; n = 100") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 16))

p_n2_250 <- results_n2 %>% dplyr::filter(n_per_group == sampsize) %>%
  plot_pvals(sampsize = sampsize, title = "Power-law in n; n = 250") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 16))

p_ls <- results_ls %>% dplyr::filter(n_per_group == sampsize) %>%
  plot_pvals(sampsize = sampsize, title = "Log-saturation") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 16))

p_slls <- results_slls %>% dplyr::filter(n_per_group == sampsize) %>%
  plot_pvals(sampsize = sampsize, title = "Std. lifted log-saturation (SLLS)") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 16))

# All four plots
(p_fact0 | p_fact01) /
  (p_n2_100 | p_n2_250) /
  (p_ls    | p_slls) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

```

## Conclusion

In this set of simulation studies we systematically evaluated different rules for defining $\varepsilon$. Our results demonstrate that:

* The **log-saturation rule** provides a stable baseline for defining $\varepsilon$ that adapts smoothly to both the observed test statistics and the sample size.
* Introducing the **lift term** mitigates anticonservative behavior for small and moderate test statistics, ensuring that the approximation remains reliable across the full range of effect sizes.
* Replacing the dependency on the maximum observed statistic $T_{\max}$ by a **permutation-based cap** makes the rule more robust to outliers and suitable for single-test settings, while still preserving the desired $\sqrt{n}$-like scaling.
* Standardization to the permutation $Z$-scale further improves comparability across different test types and scales.


## Files written

These files have been written to the target directory, ```r paste0("data/", params$name)```:

```{r list-files-target}
projthis::proj_dir_info(path_target())
```
